steps.title: Evaluate the original expression
steps.content: Use a table/regression shortcut instead of distributing every sign by hand. Enter several $t$-values and define the output list from the original expression.

steps.title: Fit a quartic
steps.content: Type $y_1\sim a t_1^4+b t_1^3+c t_1^2+d t_1+e$. Desmos returns $a=1$, $b=3$, $c=-13$, $d=4$, and $e=0$, so the expression is $t^4+3t^3-13t^2+4t$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The fitted coefficients match choice A exactly. Choice D is close but has an extra $-4$ constant; the regression shows the constant is $0$. Choice A is correct.
